       9    - Current Signals (recent launches, earnings highlights, employee sentiment, executive posts)
      10    - Interview Questions (3–7 sharp, specific questions that show I did my homework. Questions should reference the info above and connect it back to the role/team).
     … +2 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Transcribed.

✻ Sautéed for 32s

※ recap: Transcribed the interview research assistant prompt image into interviews/pre/company_research_prompt.md, verbatim with no changes. Task is complete; no next action pending unless you want edits. (disable recaps in /config)

│                       ▐▛███▜▌                      │ What's new                                                                                                                                                                                                                                │
│                      ▝▜█████▛▘                     │ Added `--bare` flag for scripted `-p` calls — skips hooks, LSP, plugin sync, and skill directory walks; requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` or an `apiKeyHelper` via `--settings` (OAuth and keychain auth disabled); auto-memory fully disabled │
│                        ▘▘ ▝▝                       │ Added `--channels` permission relay — channel servers that declare the permission capability can forward tool approval prompts to your phone                                                                                              │
│      Opus 4.8 with high effort · Claude Max ·      │ Fixed multiple concurrent Claude Code sessions requiring repeated re-authentication when one session refreshes its OAuth token                                                                                                            │

● Write(interviews/pre/company_research_prompt.md)
  ⎿  Error writing file

  Thought for 3s, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

● Write(interviews/pre/company_research_prompt.md)
  ⎿  Wrote 12 lines to interviews/pre/company_research_prompt.md
       1 # ChatGPT prompt on screen and have (prompts in description)
       2
       3 "You are acting as my interview research assistant. Below is a collection of raw notes, copy-pasted text, and links about a company. It may include their website, Wikipedia, press releases, earnings calls, Glassdoor reviews, leaked interview questions, and executive social posts."
       4
       5 Your tasks:
       6 1. Organize everything into a clean company cheat sheet to be used during interview
       7 2. Structure the output into these sections:
       8    - Company Background (origin story, founding year, mission, market position, competitors)
       9    - Current Signals (recent launches, earnings highlights, employee sentiment, executive posts)
      10    - Interview Questions (3–7 sharp, specific questions that show I did my homework. Questions should reference the info above and connect it back to the role/team).
     … +2 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Transcribed.

✻ Sautéed for 32s

※ recap: Transcribed the interview research assistant prompt image into interviews/pre/company_research_prompt.md, verbatim with no changes. Task is complete; no next action pending unless you want edits. (disable recaps in /config)

❯ great. Now read /home/v/s/g/cv/interviews/pre/company_research.typ. The prompt must reference this document explicitly at least once, and then be rewritten around it. It must be split to phases. You will also make this into a proper `SKILL`, that I'll be able to link directly to ~/.claude/skills/.
  Oh, and it should do nothing and request data if it wasn't given any, and can't infer:
  - position listing link
  - link to interviewer findable there or provided separately (or explicit mention that we don't have it)
  - point to the dir, company research and materials should go under. Within it, agent gets to create/claim `tmp/` sub-directory, and all the unstructured data (and scripts for parsing/analyzing it) get dumped there.

  The first phase will start by pulling the job description into `job_description.md` in the owned tmp/ dir, then all the company info pulled and organized (an agent per channel we're collecting data from (+ one for interviewer), - parallelize).
  Phase two: analyze the company data we got (and pull more if needed for any of the inferrence we're doing here), - and determine the key points I outlined in the linked document (on you to create a good list of actions here)
  Phase three: compile questions.

  Thought for 1m 51s, read 1 file, listed 2 directories (ctrl+o to expand)

● Now I understand the setup. There's already company_research_prompt.typ holding the prompt, and company_research.typ is the spec doc. Let me build the skill.

  ⎿  total 8
     drwxr-xr-x 2 v users 4096 Jul 10 15:19 .
     drwxr-xr-x 3 v users 4096 Jul 10 15:19 ..
     lrwxrwxrwx 1 v users   23 Jul 10 15:19 company_research.typ -> ../company_research.typ

● Write(interviews/pre/company_research/SKILL.md)
  ⎿  Wrote 55 lines to interviews/pre/company_research/SKILL.md
       2 name: company-research
       3 description: Prep for a specific job interview — research the company and the interviewer, then compile a scannable cheat sheet plus sharp questions to ask. Trigger when the user points at a job listing / says they have an interview coming up and wants company research, an interview cheat sheet, or ques
         tions to ask the interviewer.
       4 ---
       5
       6 # Company Research
       7
       8 You are the user's interview research assistant. Goal: turn a specific job opening into a scannable mid-interview cheat sheet and a set of sharp, homework-proving questions.
       9
      10 **The spec for *what* to extract lives in `./company_research.typ`. Read it first — its "Key Points" and "Pre-compile questions" sections drive Phases 2 and 3. This skill defines the *process*; that document defines the *content*.**
     … +47 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Now update the == Automate link in the spec doc to point at the skill instead of the old prompt file:

● Update(interviews/pre/company_research.typ)
  ⎿  Added 1 line, removed 1 line
      57  - If during research found some overlap between us, - mention it.
      58
      59  == Automate
      60 -[Prompt](./company_research_prompt.md)
      60 +[Skill](./company_research/SKILL.md)

  Thought for 9s (ctrl+o to expand)

● Done. Layout:

  interviews/pre/company_research/
    SKILL.md                 # phased process, gates on the 3 inputs
    company_research.typ ->  # symlink to ../company_research.typ (the content spec)

  The symlink means when you link the whole skill dir into ~/.claude/skills/, ./company_research.typ still resolves to the real repo doc — single source of truth, survives symlinking.

  Link it:
  ln -s /home/v/s/g/cv/interviews/pre/company_research ~/.claude/skills/company-research

  SKILL.md references company_research.typ explicitly as the content spec, gates on the three required inputs (listing link / interviewer-or-explicit-none / target dir), owns tmp/, and runs Phase 1 gather (job desc → parallel agent-per-channel + interviewer) → Phase 2 analyze against the doc's Key Points → Phase 3
  compile cheat sheet + filled-in questions.

  skipped: the old company_research_prompt.typ left as-is (your source doc); repoint or delete it if you want. The .typ's Automate link now points at the skill.

✻ Worked for 2m 40s

Next: symlink the skill dir into ~/.claude/skills/ to activate it. (disable recaps in
  /config)
